2605.10075.title: Active Testing of Large Language Models via Approximate Neyman Allocation
2605.10075.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) require reliable evaluation from pre-training to test-time scaling, making evaluation a recurring rather than one-off cost. As model scales grow and target tasks increasingly demand expert annotators, both the compute and labeling costs needed for each evaluation rise rapidly. Active testing aims to alleviate this bottleneck by approximating the evaluation result from a small but informative subset of the evaluation pool. However, existing approaches primarily target classification and break down on generative tasks. We introduce a novel active testing algorithm tailored to generative tasks. Our method leverages semantic entropy from surrogate models to stratify the evaluation pool and then conducts approximate Neyman allocation based on signals extracted from these surrogates. Across multiple language and multimodal benchmarks and a range of surrogate-target model pairs, our method significantly improves on baselines and closely tracks Oracle-Neyman, delivering up to 28\% MSE reduction over Uniform Sampling and an average of 22.9\% budget savings.
2605.10075.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）需要從預訓練到測試時間擴展的可靠評估，使得評估成為一項持續而非一次性的成本。隨著模型規模的增長和目標任務對專家標註者的需求日益增加，每次評估所需的計算和標註成本迅速上升。主動測試旨在通過從一個小而信息豐富的評估池子中近似評估結果來緩解這一瓶頸。然而，現有的方法主要針對分類，並在生成任務上失效。我們提出了一種針對生成任務的新型主動測試算法。我們的方法利用代理模型中的語義熵來對評估池進行分層，然後根據從這些代理中提取的信號進行近似的奈曼分配。在多個語言和多模態基準以及一系列代理-目標模型對中，我們的方法顯著改善了基線，並緊密跟踪Oracle-Neyman，相比於均勻抽樣實現了高達28%的均方誤差減少和平均22.9%的預算節省。
2605.10075.author: Zeli Liu et.al.
2605.10075.authors: Zeli Liu, Jiancheng Zhang, Cong Liu, Yinglun Zhu
